zh.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury是Edward de Salisbury III the Younger和Adeliza Raimes的女兒，出生在英國維爾爾索爾茲伯里。她和Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville至少有5個兒子和3個女兒。她去世於1215年7月13日。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Salisbury
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name primarily from Salesbury in Blackburn (Lancashire) but also occasionally from Salisbury (Wiltshire). The Lancashire placename derives from Old English salh ‘willow, sallow’ + burg ‘fortress’, while the Wiltshire placename arises from a shortened form of the Celtic placename Sorviodunum (from an unknown initial element + Celtic dūno- ‘fort’). In the Old English period the second element was dropped and Sorvio- (of unexplained etymology) became Searo- in Old English as the result of folk etymological association the Old English word searu ‘trick’; to this an explanatory burh ‘fortress, manor, town’ was added. The city is recorded in the Domesday Book as Sarisberie; the change of -r- to -l- is the result of later dissimilation.

variants.name: Salsbury
meanings: Jewish (American): adoption of the English surname (see 1 above) in place of Salomon or some other Jewish name beginning with Sa-.

nameMeanings.name: Léonelle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antimo, Ermanno, Remo, Sal.
Italian (Leonelli, Leonetti): patronymic or plural form of Leonello, a pet form of Leo or Leone .

variants.name: Leo
meanings: From a Late Latin personal name, meaning ‘lion’, which was borne by a large number of early Christian saints, most notably  Pope Leo the Great  (  ?390–461 ).

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

en.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury was born in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England as the daughter of Edward de Salisbury III the Younger and Adeliza Raimes. She had at least 5 sons and 3 daughters with Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville. She died on 13 July 1215.

fr.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury est née à Salisbury, Wiltshire, Angleterre, fille d’Edward de Salisbury III the Younger et Adeliza Raimes. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles avec Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville. Elle est décédée le 13 juillet 1215.

de.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury wurde in Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, als Tochter von Edward de Salisbury III the Younger und Adeliza Raimes geboren. Sie hatte mit Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 13. Juli 1215.

it.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury è nata il nel Salisbury, Wiltshire, England come figlia di Edward de Salisbury III the Younger e Adeliza Raimes. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie con Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville. Morì il 13 luglio 1215.

ja.lifeSummary: Léonelle of SalisburyはイギリスウィルトシュアイアサリスブリーでEdward de Salisbury III the YoungerとAdeliza Raimesの娘として生まれた。彼女はRobert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuvilleとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1215年7月13日に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury(은)는 영국 윌츠히르 살리스버이에서 Edward de Salisbury III the Younger(와)과 Adeliza Raimes의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1215년 7월 13일 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury nasceu em Salisbury, Wiltshire, Inglaterra filha de Edward de Salisbury III the Younger e Adeliza Raimes. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas com Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville. Ela faleceu em 13 de julho de 1215.

ru.lifeSummary: Léonelle of Salisbury родилась в местности салисбури, уилцхир, Англия. Её родителей звали Edward de Salisbury III the Younger и Adeliza Raimes. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери от Robert IV d'Estouteville, Baron de Cleuville. Она умерла 13 июля 1215 г.
